response:
In simple terms, gravity is the invisible pull that makes things with mass attract each other. It’s the reason why:

• You stay on the ground instead of floating away  
• The Earth orbits the Sun  
• The Moon orbits the Earth  
• Objects fall when you drop them  

Sir Isaac Newton described gravity as a force between two masses: the bigger the masses and the closer they are, the stronger the pull. Albert Einstein later explained gravity as the bending of space and time around massive objects—like a heavy ball pressing down on a trampoline, causing smaller balls to roll inward.

Whether viewed as a force or as warped spacetime, gravity governs the motion of planets, keeps our atmosphere in place, and makes everyday activities—like dropping your keys—behave predictably. That is gravity.
